fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Idaho Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Idaho - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity idaho, how to transfer physical therapist license to idaho, physical therapist compact idaho, idaho physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Idaho DOPL — Physical Therapy Licensure Board
board.parent_dept: Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses (DOPL)
board.mailing_address:
Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses
PO Box 83720
Boise ID 83720-0063

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Idaho.

steps.title: Create AccessDOPL Account and Submit Application
steps.description: Register for an account on AccessDOPL, complete the online application for a 'Physical Therapist Initial License,' and pay the required application fee of $150.

steps.title: Request Official Transcripts
steps.description: Have your official academic transcripts, verifying the completion of your accredited Physical Therapy program, sent directly from your educational institution to the Idaho Board of Physical Therapy.

steps.title: Submit NPTE Scores
steps.description: Arrange for your official National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores to be sent directly from the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) to the Idaho Board of Physical Therapy.

steps.title: Complete Idaho Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Pass the Idaho Physical Therapy Jurisprudence Examination online. This exam is typically accessed and completed after your initial application has been submitted through AccessDOPL.

steps.title: Verify Out-of-State Licenses (Endorsement Only)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, request verification of any active or inactive physical therapy licenses you hold or have held in other states. These verifications must be sent directly from the respective state boards to the Idaho Board of Physical Therapy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Idaho physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Idaho have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Idaho participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Idaho without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Idaho?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Idaho while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Idaho
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Idaho, you must complete the application process through the state's online licensing system, AccessDOPL.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Navigate to AccessDOPL at www.accessdopl.idaho.gov. 2. Click on 'Register' to create a new user account. 3. Provide your personal details, email, and create a unique username and password. 4. Once your account is created and you are logged in, select 'Apply for a New License/Registration' and then choose 'Physical Therapist Initial License' to begin your application.
